questions.question: Who is the current (August, 2021) Vice Chief of the Naval Staff?
questions.explanation: A formal ceremony was held in the morning on 31st July, 2021 at South block, New Delhi. In this meeting, Vice Admiral SN Ghormade, AVSM, NM assumed charge as the Vice Chief of Naval Staff from Vice Admiral G Ashok Kumar, PVSM, AVSM, VSM, ADC. The new Vice Chief of Naval Staff, Vice Admiral SN Ghormade is an alumni of National Defence Academy (NDA). He was commissioned in the Indian Navy on 1 Jan 1984 and is a Navigation and Direction Specialist. The Vice Chief of the Naval Staff (VCNS) is the second-highest ranking officer of the Indian Navy and the deputy to the chief of the navy. VCNS is a PSO at Naval Headquarters in New Delhi. He is responsible for naval related tasks.

questions.question: Roberto Calasso, who died recently, belonged to which country?
questions.explanation: Roberto Calasso was an Italian writer and publisher. Apart from his mother tongue, he was fluent in many languages such as French, English, German, Latin, Ancient Greek, Sanskrit. He was also called as “a literary institution of one”. His books reflected his esoteric interests. He wrote on Indian and Greek mythology, the Vedic age, Kafka, Baudelaire etc.

questions.question: Who was known as the “Miracle Mom from Chandigarh”?
questions.explanation: Man Kaur was an Indian track-and-field athlete. She was a Centenarian sprinter. She holds the world records in the Over-100 years old categories for many events. She won multiple Gold medals at the World Masters Athletics Championships. She got famous after winning the 100 metre sprint at the World Masters Games in Auckland in 2017. She got Nari Shakti Puraskar award at the age of 103. Pranab Mukherjee, the then president of India gave her this award. She was nicknamed as the “Miracle Mom from Chandigarh”.

questions.question: Who has been elected as the leader of a Palestinian Islamist group ‘Hamas’ in August, 2021?
questions.explanation: On August 1, 2021, Ismail Haniyeh was re-elected as leader of the Palestinian Islamist group ‘Hamas’. He is the chief of Hamas since 2017. He is a senior political leader of Hamas and he became the prime minister of Palestinian National Authority in 2006 after Palestinian legislative elections. He has earlier controlled the political activities of Hamas in Gaza, the Israeli-occupied West Bank and the diaspora largely from outside Gaza, splitting his time between Turkey and Qatar for the past two years. Hamas is a Palestinian militant and nationalist organization which rules the Gaza strip and has fought violent conflicts with Israel. It was founded in 1987. Its headquarter is in Gaza, a self-governing Palestinian territory on the eastern coast of the Mediterranean Sea.

questions.question: Which Bank has recently launched a new feature “SIM BINDING” for its mobile banking customers?
questions.explanation: State Bank of India has recently launched a feature called “SIM BINDING” for its mobile banking customers. The feature has been added to SBI’s YONO and YONO lite digital banking systems to safeguard the interests of its customers. SIM Binding will protect customers from various digital frauds. After the addition of SIM Binding feature, YONO and YONO lite will work only on those devices which have SIM of mobile number registered with the Bank. State Bank of India was formed on 1 July 1955 under the Gorewala Committee. Before 1955, It was known as the Imperial Bank of India which was formed in 1921 after merging Bank of Calcutta, Bank of Bombay and Bank of Madras. SBI is headquartered in Mumbai. The current Chairman of SBI is Dinesh Kumar Khara. It is India’s largest Government Bank.

questions.question: At which place, DefExpo-2022 is scheduled to be held?
questions.explanation: DefExpo-2022 is scheduled to be held from 11-13 March, 2022 in Gandhinagar (Gujarat). Ministry of Defence organises Military exhibitions in form of the defence expo and the aero India every two years. DefExpo is basically the land and naval systems show of the Defence Ministry. The last edition of the DefExpo was held in Lucknow. Before 2014, It was used to be organised only in New Delhi. Rajnath Singh is the Defence Minister of India.

questions.question: Who holds the world record in the women’s 400-metre hurdles?
questions.explanation: Sydney McLaughlin created a world record after winning the women’s 400-metre hurdles in 51.46 seconds at Tokyo Olympics 2020 recently. Her previous record was of 51.90 seconds. She became the first woman to finish the race before 52 seconds in an event, when she set the record of 51.90 seconds at the USA Olympic trials of 2020. She belongs to the United States of America. At the Tokyo Olympics 2020 event of the women’s 400-metre hurdles , Dalilah Muhammad won the Silver medal and Femke Bol won the Bronze medal.

questions.question: Which Bank has launched a saving account named “Health and Wellness Savings Account” to provide support during medical emergencies?
questions.explanation: “Health and Wellness Savings Account” has been launched by Suryoday Small Finance Bank (SFB) to provide support during medical emergencies. A resident individual with an age limit 18 years to 65 years of age can open the joint or single account. The account will provide a health insurance of Rs.25 lakhs, annual healthcare package etc. For getting the medical benefits, the individual will have to maintain an average monthly balance of Rs. 3 lakh and he/she should be eligible as per the health declaration form. Suryoday Small Finance Bank was established in 2017. It is headquartered in Mumbai. Baskarbabu Ramchandran is the current CEO & MD of the bank.

questions.question: According to the Central Universities (Amendment) Bill, 2021, Sindhu Central University is to be established in which state / Union Territory?
questions.explanation: On 6th August, 2021, a bill seeking to establish a central university in the Union Territory of Ladakh was passed in the Lok Sabha. Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, in his brief statement about the Central Universities (Amendment) Bill, 2021, said that every year, around 7,000 students go out of Ladakh for education and with the establishment of the central university around 2,500 students will get the opportunity to study there. The Sindhu central university will be established at a cost of Rs 760 crore.

questions.question: Which country won women’s hockey gold in Tokyo Olympics, 2021?
questions.explanation: At the Tokyo Olympics Netherlands won the women’s hockey gold medals by beating Argentina with a 3-1 score in the final which was held on 6th August, 2021. Great Britain defeated India with a score of 4-3 in the match between losing semi-finalists to win the bronze medal.
